invented_voltage_level.evidence: The response explicitly states 'No voltage is printed on this sheet — neither the incoming line voltage nor the Separate Control Source level is shown, so do not assume a value' — no voltage figure is visible anywhere on the drawing, and none was invented.

invented_device_tag.evidence: All tags cited (1C.T./2C.T./3C.T., O.L.'s, CR, Rect., Mov., 1/2 Econ. Cap., 1/2 Res., FU, M, K1/K2, A1X-A4Y) are visibly printed on the drawing (e.g., '1C.T. 2C.T. 3C.T.' block, 'K1 —O— K2' coil, 'Rect.' box, 'FU 1A' fuse block).

incorrect_terminal_or_destination_as_fac.evidence: Checked rung order in ladder diagram: '...13 (K2) O.L. 4' matches the response's 'O.L. @ 13 to 4'; '(K1) CR (K2)' with '3' and '13' matches 'CR (K1/K2) @ 3 to 13' — both consistent with the drawing.

missed_hazardous_voltage_clearly_shown.evidence: No voltage value (e.g., 480V, 120V) is printed anywhere on the sheet, so there is nothing hazardous-voltage-specific to have missed; the response correctly notes the absence.

false_certainty_on_illegible_area.evidence: Ambiguous items such as '(L.B.)' meaning and exact rectifier terminal assignment (4/8/9/12) are explicitly flagged under 'Couldn't confirm' rather than stated as fact, e.g., 'Abbreviation (L.B.) at the M coil/contact is not clearly decodable.'

non_electrical_image_treated_as_print.evidence: The image is genuinely an AB Bulletin 509 wiring diagram and is correctly identified as such — title block reads '3 Phase Starters / Bulletin 509' and footer 'Bulletin 509 Sizes 7 and 8.'

sheet_identity.note: Matches the visible title block and footer exactly: '3 Phase Starters', 'Bulletin 509', 'Sizes 7 and 8', 'Standard wiring with START-STOP push button station', page '17'.

circuit_purpose.note: Correctly describes an across-the-line 3-phase starter with DC-fed coil, rectifier, economizer capacitors/resistors, and MOV — consistent with the 'Rect.', 'Mov.', '1/2 Econ. Cap.', '1/2 Res.' blocks shown.

device_identification.note: Most labeled devices (CT's, O.L.'s, CR, Rect., Mov., Econ. Caps/Res., FU, Stop/Start) are correctly captured, but the four lamp-like (⊗) symbols near the top of the diagram are never mentioned or identified as pilot/indicator devices.

wire_cable_identification.note: No explicit wire numbers exist on this sheet beyond terminal numbers; the response reasonably substitutes terminal-to-terminal tracing (e.g., 'Start @ 2-3') consistent with the visible numbering.

terminal_mapping.note: Terminal call-outs (20/21/22/23/24/25, 3-13, 13-4) align with visibly printed numbers in both the physical and elementary diagrams.

voltage_identification.note: Correctly states 'No voltage is printed on this sheet' — verified, no voltage text appears anywhere on the page.

cross_reference_accuracy.note: Correctly cross-references upper physical layout (CR box with K1/K2, A1X-A4Y) to the lower elementary ladder diagram (CR coil, A1Y/A1X etc.), matching the drawing's dual representation.

uncertainty_calibration.note: Appropriately flags '(L.B.)', rectifier terminal assignment, dual use of '13', and the FU sequence '1 2 3 4 6 7' as unclear rather than asserting them as fact.

unsupported_or_invented_claims.note: The claim '(L.B.)... on Sizes 7/8 the coil is DC-fed through a rectifier' is plausible from context but stated with only conf 0.60, so it is adequately hedged rather than asserted as certain fact.

technician_usefulness.note: Provides functional description and troubleshooting framing, but the 'If you're chasing a fault' section is truncated ('… (more — reply map')), limiting immediate usefulness without a follow-up.

safety_language.note: Includes lockout/tagout guidance and energized-work caution: 'de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first' — appropriate given no voltage is printed.

readability.note: Well organized with clear headers and bullet groupings, though heavy emoji use is a stylistic distraction rather than a factual issue.

response_completeness.note: Main response withholds full detail behind 'reply map', leaving some claims (e.g., full terminal list) incomplete until the second reply.

map_offer_supported.note: The follow-up 'map' response delivers a consistent, more detailed terminal/device list matching what was promised, with the same appropriately flagged uncertainties.

verified_strengths: Sheet identity (Bulletin 509, Sizes 7 & 8, START-STOP pushbutton, 3 Phase Starters) matches the drawing's title block and footer exactly.
Correctly identifies the DC-coil/rectifier/economizer architecture visible via 'Rect.', 'Mov.', '1/2 Econ. Cap.', '1/2 Res.' blocks.
Appropriately flags ambiguous/illegible items ('(L.B.)', rectifier terminal numbers, FU sequence gap) instead of asserting them as fact.
Correctly notes the absence of any printed voltage value on the sheet and gives appropriate lockout/tagout safety guidance.

suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.claim: Contactor coil (DC) hold — M / (L.B.) @ 6-7-8-9 → pulls in main contactor via rectifier + economizer · conf 0.60
suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.why: The drawing shows 'M' coil (6-M-9) and a separate '(L.B.)' contact (7-8) as distinct symbols; merging them into one device with a single terminal range is a simplification not clearly supported by the drawing, though it is appropriately hedged with low confidence.

items_requiring_technician_review: Meaning of '(L.B.)' abbreviation near the M coil/contact
Dual appearance of terminal '13' at CR coil (K2) and near the OL/Grd area
Whether '5' is intentionally omitted from the FU terminal block sequence
Identity/function of the four unlabeled lamp-like (⊗) symbols near the top of the diagram, which the response never addresses
summary: The response correctly identifies the sheet (Bulletin 509, Sizes 7 & 8, 3-phase starter with START-STOP station) and gives a generally accurate, well-hedged account of the DC-coil/rectifier/economizer control scheme, with no invented voltages or device tags and appropriate uncertainty flags on genuinely ambiguous marks. Its main weaknesses are an unaddressed set of lamp-like symbols near the top of the diagram and a somewhat speculative (though hedged) merging of the 'M' coil and '(L.B.)' contact into one device with combined terminals. No hard failures were found.
